1	import { revalidateRedirects } from '@/server/payload/hooks/revalidateRedirects'
2	import { beforeSyncWithSearch } from '@/ui/web/search/beforeSync'
3	import { searchFields } from '@/ui/web/search/fieldOverrides'
4	import { formBuilderPlugin } from '@payloadcms/plugin-form-builder'
5	import { nestedDocsPlugin } from '@payloadcms/plugin-nested-docs'
6	import { redirectsPlugin } from '@payloadcms/plugin-redirects'
7	import { searchPlugin } from '@payloadcms/plugin-search'
8	import { seoPlugin } from '@payloadcms/plugin-seo'
9	import { GenerateTitle, GenerateURL } from '@payloadcms/plugin-seo/types'
10	import { FixedToolbarFeature, HeadingFeature, lexicalEditor } from '@payloadcms/richtext-lexical'
11	import { vercelBlobStorage } from '@payloadcms/storage-vercel-blob'
12	import { Plugin } from 'payload'
13	
14	import { getServerSideURL } from '@/infra/utils/getURL'
15	import { Page } from '@/payload-types'
16	
17	import { mcp as mcpPlugin } from './mcp'
18	
19	// MCP plugin - conditionally enabled based on env var or default
20	// Order: env var 'false' → env var 'true' → default to true
21	// Note: ConfigEntry check can be added in the handler for runtime flexibility
22	const mcp = process.env.MCP_ENABLED === 'false' ? null : mcpPlugin
23	
24	const generateTitle: GenerateTitle<Page> = ({ doc }) => {
25	  return doc?.title ? `${doc.title} | Payload Website Template` : 'Payload Website Template'
26	}
27	
28	const generateURL: GenerateURL<Page> = ({ doc }) => {
29	  const url = getServerSideURL()
30	
31	  return doc?.slug ? `${url}/${doc.slug}` : url
32	}
33	
34	// Vercel Blob storage plugin - required in production, optional in tests
35	// During type generation (PAYLOAD_GENERATE_TYPES=true), this is skipped
36	let vercelBlobPlugin: Plugin | null = null
37	if (process.env.PAYLOAD_GENERATE_TYPES !== 'true') {
38	  const blobToken = process.env.BLOB_READ_WRITE_TOKEN
39	
40	  // In test mode, skip the plugin if no valid token is provided
41	  // Valid tokens start with 'vercel_blob_rw_'
42	  const isValidToken = blobToken && blobToken.startsWith('vercel_blob_rw_')
43	  const isTestMode = process.env.NODE_ENV === 'test'
44	
45	  if (!blobToken && !isTestMode) {
46	    throw new Error(
47	      'BLOB_READ_WRITE_TOKEN environment variable is required. ' +
48	        'Vercel Blob storage is mandatory for this application. ' +
49	        'Please set BLOB_READ_WRITE_TOKEN in your environment configuration.',
50	    )
51	  }
52	
53	  // Only initialize the plugin if we have a valid token
54	  // In test mode with mock token, skip the plugin entirely
55	  if (isValidToken || (!isTestMode && blobToken)) {
56	    vercelBlobPlugin = vercelBlobStorage({
57	      addRandomSuffix: true,
58	      cacheControlMaxAge: 60 * 60 * 24 * 30, // 30 days — safe because addRandomSuffix guarantees unique filenames
59	      clientUploads: false,
60	      // Use proxy mode - URLs are /api/media/file/... and static handler proxies to blob
61	      // This ensures PDF viewer works (same-origin URLs) and backward compatibility
62	      collections: {
63	        media: true,
64	        'exercise-assets': true,
65	      },
66	      token: blobToken,
67	    })
68	  }
69	}
70	
71	export const plugins: Plugin[] = [
72	  redirectsPlugin({
73	    collections: ['pages'],
74	    overrides: {
75	      // @ts-expect-error - This is a valid override, mapped fields don't resolve to the same type
76	      fields: ({ defaultFields }) => {
77	        return defaultFields.map((field) => {
78	          if ('name' in field && field.name === 'from') {
79	            return {
80	              ...field,
81	              admin: {
82	                description: 'You will need to rebuild the website when changing this field.',
83	              },
84	            }
85	          }
86	          return field
87	        })
88	      },
89	      hooks: {
90	        afterChange: [revalidateRedirects],
91	      },
92	    },
93	  }),
94	  nestedDocsPlugin({
95	    collections: ['categories'],
96	    generateURL: (docs) => docs.reduce((url, doc) => `${url}/${doc.slug}`, ''),
97	  }),
98	  seoPlugin({
99	    generateTitle,
100	    generateURL,
101	  }),
102	  formBuilderPlugin({
103	    fields: {
104	      payment: false,
105	    },
106	    formOverrides: {
107	      fields: ({ defaultFields }) => {
108	        return defaultFields.map((field) => {
109	          if ('name' in field && field.name === 'confirmationMessage') {
110	            return {
111	              ...field,
112	              editor: lexicalEditor({
113	                features: ({ rootFeatures }) => {
114	                  return [
115	                    ...rootFeatures,
116	                    FixedToolbarFeature(),
117	                    HeadingFeature({ enabledHeadingSizes: ['h1', 'h2', 'h3', 'h4'] }),
118	                  ]
119	                },
120	              }),
121	            }
122	          }
123	          return field
124	        })
125	      },
126	    },
127	  }),
128	  searchPlugin({
129	    collections: ['courses', 'posts'],
130	    beforeSync: beforeSyncWithSearch,
131	    searchOverrides: {
132	      fields: ({ defaultFields }) => {
133	        return [...defaultFields, ...searchFields]
134	      },
135	    },
136	  }),
137	  ...(vercelBlobPlugin ? [vercelBlobPlugin] : []),
138	  // Only include MCP plugin when explicitly enabled
139	  ...(mcp ? [mcp] : []),
140	]
141

1	/**
2	 * Users Collection
3	 *
4	 * @fileType collection-config
5	 * @domain auth
6	 * @pattern rbac, user-owned
7	 * @ai-summary Users collection with authentication, RBAC roles, and audit hooks
8	 */
9	
10	import type { CollectionConfig } from 'payload'
11	
12	import { adminOnly } from '../../access/adminOnly'
13	import { adminOrContentEditor } from '../../access/adminOrContentEditor'
14	import { adminOrSelf } from '../../access/adminOrSelf'
15	// anyone import kept for future re-enablement of public signup
16	import { isUsersCollectionUser } from '@/server/payload/access/isUsersCollectionUser'
17	import { anyone } from '../../access/anyone'
18	import { auditRoleChange } from './hooks/auditRoleChange-hook'
19	import { createUserSettings } from './hooks/createUserSettings-hook'
20	import { ensureRoleOnSignup } from './hooks/ensureRoleOnSignup-hook'
21	import { preventLastAdminDemotion } from './hooks/preventLastAdminDemotion-hook'
22	import { ACCOUNT_ROLE_LABEL, AccountRole } from './roles'
23	
24	export const Users: CollectionConfig = {
25	  slug: 'users',
26	  access: {
27	    admin: adminOrContentEditor, // Admins and Advanced Content Editors can access the admin panel
28	    create: anyone, // Anyone can create (signup). OAuth also uses overrideAccess.
29	    delete: adminOnly, // Only admins can delete users
30	    read: adminOrSelf, // Admins can read all, users can read their own
31	    update: adminOrSelf, // Admins can update all, users can update their own
32	  },
33	  admin: {
34	    defaultColumns: ['name', 'email', 'role'],
35	    useAsTitle: 'name',
36	  },
37	  auth: {
38	    cookies: {
39	      secure: true,
40	      sameSite: 'None',
41	    },
42	  },
43	  fields: [
44	    {
45	      name: 'name',
46	      type: 'text',
47	    },
48	    {
49	      name: 'role',
50	      type: 'select',
51	      options: Object.entries(ACCOUNT_ROLE_LABEL).map(([value, label]) => ({
52	        label,
53	        value,
54	      })),
55	      defaultValue: AccountRole.Student,
56	      required: true,
57	      saveToJWT: true, // Include in JWT for fast access checks
58	      access: {
59	        // Only admins can update the role field
60	        update: ({ req: { user } }) =>
61	          isUsersCollectionUser(user) && user.role === AccountRole.Admin,
62	      },
63	      hooks: {
64	        // Enforce role='student' on signup (ignore client input)
65	        beforeChange: [ensureRoleOnSignup],
66	      },
67	      admin: {
68	        position: 'sidebar',
69	      },
70	    },
71	    // OAuth fields
72	    {
73	      name: 'googleSub',
74	      type: 'text',
75	      unique: true,
76	      index: true,
77	      admin: {
78	        readOnly: true,
79	        position: 'sidebar',
80	      },
81	    },
82	    {
83	      name: 'verifiedEmail',
84	      type: 'text',
85	      admin: {
86	        readOnly: true,
87	      },
88	    },
89	    {
90	      name: 'registrationMethod',
91	      type: 'select',
92	      options: [
93	        { label: 'Google', value: 'google' },
94	        { label: 'Email', value: 'email' },
95	      ],
96	      admin: {
97	        readOnly: true,
98	        position: 'sidebar',
99	      },
100	    },
101	    {
102	      name: 'registeredAt',
103	      type: 'date',
104	      admin: {
105	        readOnly: true,
106	      },
107	    },
108	    {
109	      name: 'googleProfile',
110	      type: 'group',
111	      fields: [{ name: 'name', type: 'text' }],
112	      admin: {
113	        readOnly: true,
114	      },
115	    },
116	    {
117	      name: 'courseEntitlements',
118	      type: 'array',
119	      label: 'Course Entitlements',
120	      admin: {
121	        description: 'Courses this user has access to',
122	      },
123	      access: {
124	        update: ({ req: { user } }) =>
125	          isUsersCollectionUser(user) && user.role === AccountRole.Admin,
126	      },
127	      fields: [
128	        {
129	          name: 'course',
130	          type: 'relationship',
131	          relationTo: 'courses',
132	          required: true,
133	          admin: { readOnly: true },
134	        },
135	        {
136	          name: 'grantMethod',
137	          type: 'select',
138	          required: true,
139	          defaultValue: 'admin',
140	          options: [
141	            { label: 'Admin Grant', value: 'admin' },
142	            { label: 'Payment', value: 'payment' },
143	            { label: 'Access Code', value: 'code' },
144	          ],
145	          admin: { readOnly: true },
146	        },
147	        {
148	          name: 'grantedAt',
149	          type: 'date',
150	          defaultValue: () => new Date().toISOString(),
151	          admin: { readOnly: true },
152	        },
153	      ],
154	    },
155	    // Chat quota fields (rolling window)
156	    {
157	      name: 'chatQuestionsUsed',
158	      type: 'number',
159	      defaultValue: 0,
160	      admin: {
161	        position: 'sidebar',
162	        description: 'Questions used in current window',
163	      },
164	    },
165	    {
166	      name: 'chatWindowStart',
167	      type: 'date',
168	      admin: {
169	        position: 'sidebar',
170	        description: 'When the current chat quota window started',
171	      },
172	    },
173	    {
174	      name: 'oauthLoginSecretEnc',
175	      type: 'text',
176	      // Encrypted secret for payload.login() - required for OAuth users
177	      // Server-side reads allowed via overrideAccess in callback
178	      access: {
179	        read: () => false, // Never exposed to client
180	        create: () => true, // Set during OAuth user creation
181	        update: () => false, // Never updatable after creation
182	      },
183	      admin: {
184	        hidden: true,
185	      },
186	    },
187	  ],
188	  hooks: {
189	    // Prevent demoting the last admin
190	    beforeChange: [preventLastAdminDemotion],
191	    // Audit trail for role changes
192	    afterChange: [auditRoleChange, createUserSettings],
193	  },
194	  timestamps: true,
195	}
196

1	# Release Management
2	
3	This project uses [semantic-release](https://github.com/semantic-release/semantic-release) for fully automated version management and release publishing.
4	
5	## Prerequisites
6	
7	**Node.js 22+ required** - semantic-release v25 requires Node.js 22 or higher. Install using nvm (`nvm install 22 && nvm use 22`) or download from [nodejs.org](https://nodejs.org/).
8	
9	## How It Works
10	
11	**Semantic-release analyzes your commit messages** to automatically:
12	- Determine the next version number
13	- Generate release notes
14	- Update CHANGELOG.md
15	- Create a git tag
16	- Publish a GitHub release
17	
18	## Commit Message Format
19	
20	We use [Conventional Commits](https://www.conventionalcommits.org/) format, enforced by commitlint:
21	
22	```
23	<type>(<scope>): <subject>
24	
25	<body>
26	```
27	
28	### Commit Types & Version Bumps
29	
30	| Type | Version Bump | Example |
31	|------|--------------|---------|
32	| `feat:` | **Minor** (1.0.0 → 1.1.0) | `feat: add user profile page` |
33	| `fix:` | **Patch** (1.0.0 → 1.0.1) | `fix: repair broken login form` |
34	| `perf:` | **Patch** (1.0.0 → 1.0.1) | `perf: optimize image loading` |
35	| `docs:` | **Patch** (1.0.0 → 1.0.1) | `docs: update API documentation` |
36	| `refactor:` | **Patch** (1.0.0 → 1.0.1) | `refactor: simplify auth logic` |
37	| `build:` | **Patch** (1.0.0 → 1.0.1) | `build: update webpack config` |
38	| `feat!:` or `BREAKING CHANGE:` | **Major** (1.0.0 → 2.0.0) | `feat!: redesign authentication system` |
39	
40	**No version bump:**
41	- `ci:` - CI configuration changes
42	- `chore:` - Maintenance tasks
43	- `test:` - Adding/updating tests
44	- `style:` - Code formatting (no logic changes)
45	
46	### Breaking Changes
47	
48	To trigger a major version bump, use one of these:
49	
50	**Option 1: Exclamation mark**
51	```
52	feat!: redesign user authentication
53	
54	BREAKING CHANGE: Authentication now requires OAuth 2.0
55	```
56	
57	**Option 2: Footer**
58	```
59	feat: add new payment provider
60	
61	BREAKING CHANGE: PaymentService.process() now returns Promise instead of callback
62	```
63	
64	## Release Workflow
65	
66	### Automatic Releases (Recommended)
67	
68	1. **Develop on `dev` branch** with conventional commits
69	2. **Merge to `main`** (manually or via automated weekly merge)
70	3. **GitHub Actions automatically runs** semantic-release
71	4. **Release is published** if there are releasable commits
72	
73	```
74	dev branch → merge to main → semantic-release runs → release created
75	```
76	
77	### Manual Trigger
78	
79	You can manually trigger a release from the GitHub Actions UI:
80	1. Go to **Actions** → **Release** workflow
81	2. Click **Run workflow**
82	3. Select `main` branch
83	4. Click **Run workflow**
84	
85	## Testing Releases Locally
86	
87	Before pushing, test what semantic-release will do:
88	
89	```bash
90	# Dry run - see what would happen without making changes
91	pnpm release:dry-run
92	```
93	
94	This will show:
95	- What version would be released
96	- What commits would be included
97	- What the changelog would look like
98	
99	## Release Artifacts
100	
101	Each release creates:
102	
103	1. **Git Tag** - `v1.2.3` format
104	2. **GitHub Release** - with auto-generated release notes
105	3. **Updated CHANGELOG.md** - committed to the repository
106	4. **Updated package.json** - version bumped automatically
107	
108	## Examples
109	
110	### Feature Release (Minor Version)
111	
112	```bash
113	# Commits on dev
114	git commit -m "feat: add user dashboard
115	
116	Adds a new dashboard page with user statistics and activity overview."
117	
118	git commit -m "feat: add export functionality
119	
120	Users can now export their data as CSV or JSON."
121	
122	# Merge to main → semantic-release runs
123	# Result: 1.0.0 → 1.1.0
124	```
125	
126	### Bug Fix Release (Patch Version)
127	
128	```bash
129	git commit -m "fix: resolve login timeout issue
130	
131	Increases session timeout from 5 to 15 minutes to prevent
132	unexpected logouts during active sessions."
133	
134	# Merge to main → semantic-release runs
135	# Result: 1.1.0 → 1.1.1
136	```
137	
138	### Breaking Change (Major Version)
139	
140	```bash
141	git commit -m "feat!: redesign authentication system
142	
143	BREAKING CHANGE: All users must re-authenticate after this update.
144	The new system uses OAuth 2.0 instead of session-based auth."
145	
146	# Merge to main → semantic-release runs
147	# Result: 1.1.1 → 2.0.0
148	```
149	
150	## Configuration
151	
152	### semantic-release Configuration
153	
154	Configuration is in [`.releaserc.json`](../.releaserc.json):
155	
156	- **Branches:** Only `main` triggers releases
157	- **Plugins:**
158	  - `commit-analyzer` - Determines version from commits
159	  - `release-notes-generator` - Creates release notes
160	  - `changelog` - Updates CHANGELOG.md
161	  - `npm` - Updates package.json (no publish to npm)
162	  - `git` - Commits changes and creates tags
163	  - `github` - Creates GitHub releases
164	
165	### GitHub Actions Workflow
166	
167	The release workflow ([`.github/workflows/release.yml`](../.github/workflows/release.yml)):
168	
169	- **Triggers:** Push to `main` or manual dispatch
170	- **Skips:** Commits with `[skip ci]` (prevents infinite loops)
171	- **Permissions:** Needs write access to create releases and tags
172	
173	## Troubleshooting
174	
175	### No Release Created
176	
177	**Possible reasons:**
178	- No releasable commits since last release
179	- Only commits with types that don't trigger releases (ci, chore, test, style)
180	- All commits since last release were already released
181	
182	**Solution:** Check commit history and ensure you're using releasable commit types.
183	
184	### Release Failed
185	
186	**Check:**
187	1. GitHub Actions logs for error details
188	2. Ensure `GITHUB_TOKEN` has correct permissions
189	3. Verify no conflicts in CHANGELOG.md or package.json
190	4. Run `pnpm release:dry-run` locally to debug
191	
192	### Dependabot PRs
193	
194	Dependabot automatically creates PRs with conventional commits:
195	- `build(deps): bump package-name` → triggers patch release
196	- You can merge multiple dependency PRs before releasing
197	
198	## Best Practices
199	
200	1. **Write clear commit messages** - They become your release notes
201	2. **Use conventional commits** - Required for semantic-release
202	3. **Batch related changes** - Group related features in one commit
203	4. **Test before merging to main** - Main branch should always be releasable
204	5. **Review generated changelog** - Check GitHub releases page after deployment
205	6. **Use dry-run locally** - Test what will happen before pushing
206	
207	## Related Documentation
208	
209	- [Conventional Commits](https://www.conventionalcommits.org/)
210	- [Semantic Versioning](https://semver.org/)
211	- [semantic-release Documentation](https://semantic-release.gitbook.io/)
212	- [Project Commitlint Config](../commitlint.config.js):
213

1	# Brain Server — Making It Fully Operational
2	
3	## Current State ✅
4	
5	The brain server **is accepting prompts and responding**:
6	
7	```
8	Connected to brain at http://100.66.248.120:4097/sse
9	📋 Available tools (17):
10	  - get_context_tree
11	  - semantic_identifier_search
12	  - get_file_skeleton
13	  - semantic_code_search
14	  - get_blast_radius
15	  - run_static_analysis
16	  - semantic_navigate
17	  - search_memory_graph
18	  - retrieve_with_traversal
19	  - upsert_memory_node
20	  - create_relation
21	  - add_interlinked_context
22	  - ...and more
23	```
24	
25	## What Works
26	
27	1. ✅ Brain server accepts SSE connections
28	2. ✅ Brain server provides 17 MCP tools
29	3. ✅ Tool calls return meaningful data
30	4. ✅ `scripts/cody/brain-client.ts` can connect and call tools
31	
32	## What's Needed
33	
34	### 1. Verify Claude API Integration Works
35	
36	The brain-client.ts I wrote uses Claude API with MCP tools. Need to verify:
37	- Does Claude API key exist on the machine?
38	- Does the tool-use loop work end-to-end?
39	
40	```bash
41	# Test with ANTHROPIC_API_KEY set
42	ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=sk-ant-... npx tsx scripts/cody/brain-test.ts
43	```
44	
45	### 2. Create a Brain CLI for Manual Testing
46	
47	A simple CLI to send prompts to the brain:
48	
49	```bash
50	pnpm brain "Explain the auth service"
51	pnpm brain "What files handle user authentication?"
52	pnpm brain "Find similar code to payment processing"
53	```
54	
55	### 3. Wire Brain into Pipeline (Already Done ✅)
56	
57	The code I wrote in Phase 2:
58	- `entry.ts` routes 'full' mode to `runBrainFullMode`
59	- `runBrainFullMode` calls `runArchitectBrain` → `runReviewBrain`
60	- Falls back to standard pipeline if brain unavailable
61	
62	### 4. Test End-to-End Pipeline with Brain
63	
64	```bash
65	# Set brain URL
66	export BRAIN_SERVER_URL=http://100.66.248.120:4097/sse
67	
68	# Run a test task
69	pnpm cody --task-id 260320-brain-test --mode full
70	```
71	
72	## Implementation Plan
73	
74	### Step 1: Create Brain CLI (30 min)
75	
76	Create `scripts/cody/brain-cli.ts` for manual testing:
77	
78	```typescript
79	// Accepts prompts, sends to brain, prints response
80	// Useful for verifying brain works before running pipeline
81	```
82	
83	### Step 2: Test Claude API Integration (1 hour)
84	
85	Run the full tool-use loop with actual Claude API:
86	
87	```bash
88	ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=sk-ant-... BRAIN_SERVER_URL=http://100.66.248.120:4097/sse \
89	  npx tsx scripts/cody/brain-test.ts
90	```
91	
92	### Step 3: Create Test Task (1 hour)
93	
94	Create a simple task to test brain pipeline:
95	
96	```bash
97	pnpm cody --task-id 260320-brain-test --mode full --file docs/brain-server/05-claude-mcp-option.md
98	```
99	
100	### Step 4: Verify Build Stage Uses Brain Tools (1 hour)
101	
102	Check that OpenCode can access brain tools via `opencode.json` MCP config.
103	
104	## Brain Server Management Commands
105	
106	### Restart Brain
107	```bash
108	ssh root@184.174.39.227 "cd /opt/brain-server && docker-compose restart brain"
109	```
110	
111	### View Logs
112	```bash
113	ssh root@184.174.39.227 "docker logs -f brain-server-brain-1"
114	```
115	
116	### Check Resource Usage
117	```bash

1	# Brain Server — Requirements Setup
2	
3	Step-by-step checklist to get all accounts, keys, and access ready before implementation.
4	
5	---
6	
7	## Step 1: Hetzner Account
8	
9	- [ ] Create account at https://www.hetzner.com/cloud
10	- [ ] Add payment method (credit card or PayPal)
11	- [ ] Provision server: CX32 (4 vCPU, 8GB RAM, 80GB SSD, ~$7/mo)
12	  - Location: Choose closest datacenter (EU)
13	  - OS: Ubuntu 24.04
14	  - SSH key: Add your existing key or generate new one
15	- [ ] Note the server IP
16	
17	## Step 2: SSH Access
18	
19	- [ ] Generate SSH key (if needed): `ssh-keygen -t ed25519 -C "brain-server"`
20	- [ ] Add public key to Hetzner during provisioning
21	- [ ] Verify SSH access: `ssh root@<server-ip>`
22	
23	## Step 3: Tailscale
24	
25	- [ ] Already have Tailscale account (used for remote-agent)
26	- [ ] Install Tailscale on VPS: `curl -fsSL https://tailscale.com/install.sh | sh`
27	- [ ] Join tailnet: `tailscale up --authkey=<authkey>`
28	- [ ] Note the Tailscale IP
29	- [ ] Verify from Mac: `ping <tailscale-ip>`
30	
31	## Step 4: Tailscale Auth Key for CI
32	
33	- [ ] Go to Tailscale admin: Settings > Keys
34	- [ ] Generate auth key (ephemeral, reusable, tagged `ci`)
35	- [ ] Add as GitHub secret: `TAILSCALE_AUTHKEY`
36	- [ ] Add `tailscale-action` to CI workflow (test in a separate PR first)
37	
38	## Step 5: GitHub Webhook
39	
40	- [ ] Generate webhook secret: `openssl rand -hex 32`
41	- [ ] Save secret on VPS as env var
42	- [ ] Go to GitHub repo > Settings > Webhooks > Add webhook
43	  - Payload URL: `http://<tailscale-ip>:9000/hooks/repo-sync`
44	  - Content type: `application/json`
45	  - Secret: the generated secret
46	  - Events: Just the `push` event
47	  - Active: Yes
48	
49	## Step 6: Docker on VPS
50	
51	- [ ] SSH into VPS
52	- [ ] Install Docker:
53	  ```bash
54	  apt-get update && apt-get install -y docker.io docker-compose-v2
55	  systemctl enable docker
56	  ```
57	- [ ] Verify: `docker run hello-world`
58	
59	## Step 7: Clone Repo on VPS
60	
61	- [ ] Generate deploy key (read-only): `ssh-keygen -t ed25519 -C "brain-server-deploy"`
62	- [ ] Add public key to GitHub repo: Settings > Deploy keys (read-only)
63	- [ ] Clone: `git clone git@github.com:A-Guy-educ/A-Guy.git /opt/repo`
64	- [ ] Verify: `ls /opt/repo/src/`
65	
66	## Step 8: Verify API Keys
67	
68	- [ ] Anthropic API key available (already used by pipeline): `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY`
69	- [ ] Add to VPS env (for architect brain): stored in `/opt/brain-server/.env`
70	
71	## Step 9: Firewall
72	
73	- [ ] Block all public ports except SSH (22):
74	  ```bash
75	  ufw default deny incoming
76	  ufw allow ssh
77	  ufw enable
78	  ```
79	- [ ] All other traffic goes through Tailscale (no public exposure)
80	
81	---
82	
83	## Verification Checklist
84	
85	After completing all steps, verify:
86	
87	- [ ] Can SSH into VPS from Mac
88	- [ ] Can reach VPS via Tailscale IP from Mac
89	- [ ] Docker runs on VPS
90	- [ ] Repo is cloned and accessible at `/opt/repo`
91	- [ ] Webhook fires on push (check GitHub delivery logs)
92	- [ ] Tailscale auth key works in CI (test with a dummy workflow)
93	
94	---
95	
96	## Estimated Time: 1-2 hours
97

payload.text:
Now I have a good understanding. There's a `PricingPlans` collection that defines pricing for lessons with PayPal/Stripe/Manual options, and there's a remote branch `1516-payment-system-integration`. The Users collection has `courseEntitlements` with a `grantMethod: 'payment'` option.

Let me check if there's anything in the environment variables or if there's more context about what the payment goal is supposed to accomplish.

There's no Stripe or PayPal code yet. The `PricingPlans` collection exists but there's no actual payment integration. The goal "payment" likely refers to implementing the payment system integration (with Stripe/PayPal) for course purchases.

Let me now propose a task list based on what I've found:
1. The PricingPlans collection exists (defines pricing)
2. Users have courseEntitlements with grantMethod: 'payment' 
3. But there's no actual Stripe/PayPal integration code

The payment system integration would likely involve:
1. Setting up Stripe SDK/environment
2. Creating checkout sessions
3. Webhook handlers for payment confirmation
4. Linking payments to course entitlements
5. Admin UI for managing payment status

Let me propose 5-7 tasks for this.

payload.content:
Based on my exploration, the codebase has a `PricingPlans` collection (PayPal/Stripe/Manual providers) and `courseEntitlements` with `grantMethod: 'payment'`, but **no actual payment integration code exists**. The branch `1516-payment-system-integration` confirms this is planned work.

Here's my proposed task list for the "payment" goal:

---

## Proposed Tasks: Payment System Integration

| # | Task | Description |
|---|------|-------------|
| 1 | **Configure payment provider SDKs** | Install and configure Stripe SDK and PayPal SDK packages; add required environment variables (`STRIPE_SECRET_KEY`, `STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET`, `PAYPAL_CLIENT_ID`, `PAYPAL_CLIENT_SECRET`) |
| 2 | **Create payment service layer** | Build `src/lib/payments/` with provider-agnostic `PaymentProvider` interface and Stripe/PayPal implementations; handle checkout session creation and payment status checks |
| 3 | **Implement checkout API endpoint** | Create `POST /api/payments/checkout` endpoint that accepts `pricingPlanId` and user ID, creates a checkout session with the selected provider |
| 4 | **Build payment webhook handlers** | Implement `POST /api/payments/stripe-webhook` and `POST /api/payments/paypal-webhook` to handle payment confirmation events and grant course entitlements |
| 5 | **Add payment status endpoint** | Create `GET /api/payments/status/:sessionId` to let users check payment/entitlement status |
| 6 | **Create admin payment management UI** | Add payment management UI in admin panel to view payments, manually grant entitlements, and handle refunds |
| 7 | **Write integration tests** | Cover checkout flow, webhook processing, and entitlement granting with integration tests |

---

**Approve these tasks?** Reply with changes or "approve" to proceed to Pass 2 (implementation planning).
